title: malcolmia binervis subsp. confusa
intro: Malcolmia binervis subsp. confusa, also known as 'whitlow grass', is a type of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Malcolmia binervis subsp. confusa include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable herb for topical applications. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing its leaves and flowers in oil or water to create a medicated paste or tincture for treating various skin conditions.
